sections.title: Overview
sections.raw_text:
WaiverPro is a Healthcare Waiver Management platform that streamlines regulatory waiver processes,
from submission through approval. Facilities use WaiverPro to apply for and track three categories of
waivers: Patient Needs Waiver (PNW), Program Flexibility (PF), and Workforce Shortage Waiver
(WSW).

sections.title: Accessing WaiverPro
sections.raw_text:
Users access their WaiverPro account by navigating to the login page and entering their registered
email address and password. The login button should be labeled 'Sign In' to align with platform-wide
terminology used across all WaiverPro touchpoints.
The email field placeholder should display the example format 'name@company.com' to reinforce that
business email addresses are required for registration.

sections.title: Patient Needs Waiver (PNW) Request
sections.raw_text:
Facilities may apply when seeking a waiver of the 24-hour CNA requirement to meet individual patient
care plan modifications based on clinical needs. Applications require documented patient or family
request for accommodation modification, along with clinical assessment supporting that the
modification must be care-specific. Interdisciplinary team agreement and approval is required prior to
submission.
Processing Time: 10-20 business days. Waiver Period: 6-12 months.

sections.title: Program Flexibility (PF) Request
sections.raw_text:
Facilities seeking program flexibility from regulations must include justification for the program flexibility
being sought, an adequate description of the alternative measures, and documentation supporting that
patient care will not be compromised. Facilities may request program flexibility for a limited term with a
start and end date; applications submitted more than one year in advance will not be accepted.
Processing Time: Within 60 days. Waiver Period: 6-12 months.

sections.title: Workforce Shortage Waiver (WSW) Request
sections.raw_text:
Facilities seeking a waiver of the 3:1 direct care hour requirement due to a documented shortage of
available and appropriate health care professionals and direct care givers may apply. Facilities may
only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same
year. Applications must be submitted within the annual application window of January 1 - April 30.
Only licensed freestanding SNFs (excluding district, state-owned hospitals and developmental centers)
may apply. Approved facilities must include an expiration date and specific terms; renewal may be
required if conditions are not met.

sections.title: My Applications Dashboard
sections.raw_text:
Once logged in, users land on the 'My Applications' dashboard, which displays a list of all waiver
applications submitted by the facility, along with their current status (e.g. Pending, Approved, Denied,
Expired). Each application entry should display the waiver type, submission date, and a unique
application reference number.

sections.title: User Management
sections.raw_text:
Administrators can manage facility user accounts from the User Management page, including adding
new users, editing roles and permissions, and deactivating accounts that should no longer have
access. All role changes must be logged for audit purposes.

sections.title: Support and Contact Information
sections.raw_text:
Users requiring assistance can reach the WaiverPro support team via the Contact page. The official
support email listed should be support@waiverpro.com and the support phone line should be
available Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM.

sections.title: Privacy Policy and Terms of Service
sections.reference: Section 9: Privacy Policy and Terms of Service
sections.raw_text:
WaiverPro's Privacy Policy and Terms of Service are accessible from the footer of every page,
including the public landing page. These documents must be reviewed and accepted during account
registration.

rules.rule_text: The login page should be accessible.
The login page should have a 'Sign In' button.
The email field should have a placeholder displaying 'name@company.com'.
The email address entered should be a business email address.
The password field should be present on the login page.
Facilities must apply for a waiver of the 24-hour CNA requirement.
Applications require a documented patient or family request for accommodation modification.
Applications require a clinical assessment supporting care-specific modification.
Interdisciplinary team agreement is required prior to submission.
Interdisciplinary team approval is required prior to submission.
Processing time for waiver applications is 10-20 business days.
Waiver period is 6-12 months.
Facilities must include justification for the program flexibility being sought.
Facilities must provide an adequate description of the alternative measures.
Facilities must document that patient care will not be compromised.
Applications submitted more than one year in advance will not be accepted.
Processing of program flexibility requests must be completed within 60 days.
The waiver period for program flexibility must be between 6-12 months.
Facilities must have a documented shortage of available and appropriate health care professionals and direct care givers.
Facilities may only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same year.
Applications must be submitted within the annual application window of January 1 - April 30.
Only licensed freestanding SNFs (excluding district, state-owned hospitals and developmental centers) may apply.
Approved facilities must include an expiration date.
Approved facilities must include specific terms.
Approved facilities may require renewal if conditions are not met.
The 'My Applications' dashboard is displayed after logging in.
The dashboard displays a list of all waiver applications submitted by the facility.
Each application entry displays the waiver type.
Each application entry displays the submission date.
Each application entry displays a unique application reference number.
The dashboard displays the current status of each waiver application (e.g. Pending, Approved, Denied, Expired).
Administrators can manage facility user accounts from the User Management page.
Administrators can add new users.
Administrators can edit roles.
Administrators can edit permissions.
Administrators can deactivate accounts.
All role changes must be logged.
The official support email is support@waiverpro.com.
The support phone line is available Monday through Friday.
The support phone line is available from 9 AM to 5 PM.
